rulings.Case Title: RANDALL WHITNEY VS. METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Randall Whitney'S Demurrer To The Amended Answer Of Respondent Metropolitan Transportation Commission
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 1, Petitioner Randall Whitney'S Demurrer To The Amended Answer Of Respondent Metropolitan Transportation Commission Petitioner Randall Whitney's demurrer to all five affirmative defenses alleged in the amended answer filed by respondent Metropolitan Transportation Commission is overruled as to all five affirmative defenses. All five affirmative defenses are adequately alleged because it is at least theoretically possible that MTC could prevail on any of them and each of them is a potential bar to some or all of the relief sought by Mr. Whitney. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for MTC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: RANDALL WHITNEY VS. METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION COMMISSION's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Respondent Metropolitan Transportation Commission's motion for judgment on the pleadings on the first three causes of action in the petition filed by Randall Whitney is denied as to all three causes of action. The recent decision of Templo v. State of California (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 730 requires denial of MTC's motion. The plaintiffs in Templo alleged a claim that a state statute was unconstitutional because it constituted an improper "tax" and was not enacted by the two-thirds vote required by Article XIII of the California Constituiton and named the State of California as a defendant on that claim. The State filed a motion for judgment on the pleadings as to that claim on the grounds that the state agency that administers the program established by the statute, not the State, is the proper defendant on the claim. In holding that the State was not the proper defendant and the state agency who administered the statute was the proper defendant, the court of appeal, quoting a California Supreme Court case, stated that "in cases challenging the constitutionality of a statute, the interest of the Legislature or the Governor 'is not of the immediacy and directness requisite to party status.' Rather, state and local agencies with 'direct instituional interest' are the proper defendants." (214 Cal. App. 4th at 736, quoting Serrano v. Priest (1976) 18 Cal. 3d 728, 752 (internal citation omitted)). Per Templo, MTC, which acknowledges that it is tasked with overseeing and funding the programs resulting from the increased revenue from SB595 and RM3, is a proper defendant on Mr. Whitney's claims seeking to establish the invalidity of SB595 and RM3. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Whitney is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/hk

rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. BETTY YEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Thrivent Financial For Lutherans For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS' Motion Of Plaintiff Thrivent Financial For Lutherans For Attorneys Fees Hearing required unless defendants request to continue the hearing to a date other than a Friday on or before January 17, 2019 in light of the remainder of this tentative ruling. Per Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal App. 4th 1522, the court exercises its discretion to consider the declaration of Mr. Seybold submitted with the reply papers. Because the court will consider Mr. Seybold's declaration, defendants are entitled to respond in writing to that declaration and the court is amenable to continuing the hearing to give defendants sufficient time to file supplemental opposition papers directed solely to Mr. Seybold's declaration and the arguments made in the reply memorandum about that declaration. If defendants do not desire a continuance of the hearing, at the hearing counsel for both sides should be prepared to explain, with reference to specific admissible evidence, their views why or why not the exhibits to Mr. Seybold's declaration provide sufficient evidence to satisfy Thrivent's burden of showing that the summary judgment decision conferred a significant benefit on other life insurers and/or on insureds of other life insurers. If defendants desire a continuance of the hearing, counsel should meet and confer on a schedule and no later than 5pm on December 17, 2018 counsel for defendants must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Thrivent stating the agreed dates for the filing of supplemental opposition papers and the hearing or stating the respective positions of the parties as to those dates.=302/hk

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANTS TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD and ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY USA CO., LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants Talesun Solar USA Ltd. and Zhongli New Energy USA Co., LLC's motion to strike the second amended complaint is granted. rulings.Case Title: CLARE SPRINGS VS. WELLS FRAGO INSURANCE SERVICES USA, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response And Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANTs WELLS FARGO INSURANCE SERVICES USA, INC, DUSTIN KLASS, and DONALD GRAY'S Motion To Compel Further Response And Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The trust documents are relevant. Plaintiff, as party claiming the attorney-client privilege, has the burden of establishing the preliminary facts necessary to support its claim. Plaintiff has not met his burden because plaintiff has not produced a privilege log or sufficient factual information for other parties to evaluate the merits of that claim. CCP 2031.240(c),(1). Within 20 days, plaintiff shall produce a privilege log with sufficient details as to the underlying information to meet the preliminary factual showing required to support the privilege claim; otherwise, plaintiff shall provide amended Code-compliant responses and documents. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN CLOPTON VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KAREN CLOPTON's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted as to Requests for Production Nos. 39 and 54. The motion is granted, in part, as to RFP Nos. 6, 10, and 47 as follows: No. 6 (plaintiff's job performance), the scope is narrowed to include the individuals responsible for evaluating plaintiff's job performance, without prejudice to broaden the scope later if there is good cause; No. 10, the scope is narrowed to include individuals who made or approved the termination decision, without prejudice to broaden the scope later if there is good cause; and, No. 47, the request is limited to African-American management-level employees of CPUC. The motion is denied as to RFP Nos. 3, 11, 14, 16, 18-22, 24, 25, 28, 31-35, 40-46, 48, 50, 51, 57, 58, 60, 63, 64, 69, 72, based on defendant's supplemental responses indicating all responsive documents have been produced. The motion is denied without prejudice as to Nos. 2, 8, 9, 30, 36, 53. These requests, as drafted, are overbroad, and plaintiff filed the motion before the parties were able to reach agreement how to narrow the scope. The motion is denied without prejudice as to No. 49 (Smith memo), No. 52 (Semcer evaluation), 56 (investigation), pending defendant's production of a privilege log. Defendant shall produce a sufficiently detailed privilege log identifying any information being withheld on privilege or other grounds. Defendant shall serve supplemental verified responses, documents and the privilege log within 30 days. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA STEINHAUER ET AL VS. CHARLES E. SKOMER MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pertaining To Plaintiff Steinhauer'S Medical History; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFFs JOSHUA STEINHAUER and PATTY GLATT's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pertaining To Plaintiff Steinhauer'S Medical History; Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff has a privacy interest in his medical information. However, defendant has demonstrated that plaintiff's medical information is relevant to the injuries put at issue by plaintiff in his complaint and discovery responses. Complaint 3, 20, 25, 48; Won Decl., Ex. 1, Nos. 6.2, 6.3. Plaintiff has not met his burden to show feasible and effective alternatives to the subpoenas which have a lesser impact on privacy interests, and plaintiff's proposals to narrow the scope are limited to only some of the issues and injuries claimed. Therefore, plaintiff may choose to limit the injuries he puts at issue. Otherwise, the motion to quash is denied with two exceptions: (1) the request for billing records is limited to October 2005 to the present, per defendant's offer; and (2) the subpoenas shall exclude plaintiff's psychological records, provided that no claim is being made for mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed, per plaintiff's offer. The production date on the reissued subpoenas will be at least 45 days to allow the parties to meet and confer regarding a stipulated protective order to limit the intrusion on plaintiff's privacy interest. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC VS. BEN ESCOBAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 18, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC's Motion To Compel Discovery Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, in part. Defendants Escobar and Evotek, Inc. shall produce verified Code-compliant responses to Requests for Production Nos. 7, 11, 13-17, 20, 21, 23 (Escobar), and Nos. 7, 9, 12, 14-21 (Evotek), and responsive documents. Defendants shall provide a sufficiently detailed privilege log for information withheld on privilege grounds. In addition, confidential information shall be produced unredacted, pursuant to the parties' double-layer protective order; redactions shall be limited to information that is both "confidential" and "not responsive" to any discovery request. Redactions, if any, shall also include a sufficiently detailed annotation per the parties' protective order. Responses are due within 30 days of notice of order. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
